floor_history.headline: fable-blitz deep recon: csmd4 IS marionation region-1's cheaper twin (no region-3) - the whole gap is the in-place-basin v1/a0 allocation-equation flip (pri(arg5) > pri(t0)); the wrap-weighting x in-p

Structural axis (decl order, s32/u32 narrow, statement re-assoc under arg5-after-t0) closed directly on csmd4: 4/4 masked-INERT at 2, head-order flip regresses to 7 - twin's structural closure confirm

Structural axis on csmd4 h5 base fully closed: 13 variants swept (var-splits, expression compression, decl-position, pointer-deref form) — 8 INERT at masked 2, 5 measured regressions (t0s-split=3, arg
Frontier top-3 cleared: do-while(0) wrap direction-invariant +2 insns (KILLS pri-lift lever), cnt-carrier regresses to masked 4, volatile fmt-carrier policy-rejected. h5 floor holds at 2.

frontier.hypothesis: The residual {sll4@54 <-> addu5@55} pair may respond to a LOCAL block-scope multi-set variable (declared inside the inline block, set twice on non-collapsing values, referenced 3-4 times) as the arg5 value-carrier — the ledger records fn-scope carriers going to s0 spill and fresh single-set locals folding, but a block-scope multi-set with a legitimately distinct second-set value (not idx[1] scrambled) has not been swept.
frontier.mechanism: combine.c merges paired sets with accurate ref bookkeeping (session-3 evidence, e5aa2dd4), but only when the sets are transformable equalities. A block-scope carrier initialised from a UNIQUE second value (e.g. the D_800A11D5 dispatch byte) would be non-mergeable and could lift arg5-qty refs without triggering combine collapse.
frontier.next_probe: Sweep {block-scope `s32 c = D_800A11D5; c = idx_1494[1]; ...`; block-scope `s32 c = *idx_1495; c = idx_1494[1]; ...`; block-scope `s32 c = t0; c = idx_1494[1]; ...`} — three distinct multi-set precedents whose first value is a live-here-not-dead reference. Measure each against the h5 base.

frontier.hypothesis: A statement-level rearrangement of the debug_printf CALL argument evaluation shape (e.g. pre-computing D_800A11DC[D_800A11D5] into a named local BEFORE the arg5 stage) shifts the arg-register homing seat and may re-time the fmt-la relative to t0's death without needing to hide the symbol from equiv_constant.
frontier.mechanism: expand.c handles CALL args right-to-left; naming an earlier arg's evaluation moves its LUID slot and may re-order the ready queue when arg5 births.
frontier.next_probe: Insert `s32 dispatch = D_800A11DC[D_800A11D5];` BEFORE the arg5 stage; use dispatch in the call. Measure. Compare vs. inserting it AFTER arg5. Compare vs. naming the fmt pointer itself as `const char *fmt = (const char *)&D_800161C8;` (non-volatile, so the symbol is CSE-visible — the change would be the LUID slot only).

frontier.hypothesis: Nested do-while(0) wraps ARE sanctioned with single-level-insufficient documentation. Given single-level wraps are direction-invariant scheduler barriers here (measured KILLED this session), nesting is unlikely to help — but a nested wrap that includes idx_1494 dereference INSIDE the outer wrap (making both loads participate in the loop-note refs) is different in kind from the single-level probes.
frontier.mechanism: flow.c multiplies REG_N_REFS by depth; nested wraps double the multiplier. If depth-2 loops both idx dereferences, cse might invalidate the equiv-sink defeat on D_800A11D5 (the pp alias is refs-2, session-1 evidence).
frontier.next_probe: Wrap the entire inline block (idx_1494[0], idx_1494[1], t0 chain, arg5 chain, pp, and the CALL) in one do{}while(0). Measure. If +N insns and masked worsens, kills the nested-wrap direction entirely. If masked improves at any insn budget, characterise the depth-2 ref reweighting.
